skill.name: summary-generator
skill.description: Generates concise lesson summaries from educational Markdown files using a structured Socratic extraction process.
skill.summary: Create concise `.summary.md` files for educational lessons.
skill.author: 92Bilal26

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer flagged Markdown backticks, C1-C2 proficiency text, and code examples as command execution, C2 language, and weak cryptography. Review found these are false positives in documentation, with the only meaningful risk being intentional filesystem reads and writes for lesson summary files.

low_findings.title: Filesystem Access Required for Summary Generation
low_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to read a full lesson file and write a `.summary.md` companion file. This is expected behavior, but users should apply it only to intended lesson paths.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The workflow directly says to read the target lesson file and write the `.summary.md` file. The behavior is legitimate for the skill purpose, but it still touches local files.

low_findings.title: Static Backtick Command Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick findings are Markdown code spans, fenced Markdown templates, file naming examples, and lesson examples. No shell command execution pattern is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All flagged backticks appear inside Markdown documentation or examples. I did not find Ruby code, shell execution, subprocess calls, or instructions to run these snippets as commands.

low_findings.title: Static High-Severity Keyword Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The C2 keyword finding is the `C1-C2` advanced proficiency label, and the weak cryptography finding comes from Markdown backticks in descriptive text. No command-and-control behavior or cryptographic implementation was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The high-severity indicators are explained by educational proficiency notation and Markdown formatting. The reviewed file contains no network endpoints, beaconing logic, encryption code, or secret handling.

content.value_statement: Long lessons are hard to review quickly after practice. This skill creates concise Markdown summaries that highlight concepts, patterns, mistakes, and connections.
actual_capabilities: Extracts one core concept from a lesson.
Identifies two or three reusable mental models.
Lists practical patterns, workflows, or techniques.
Adds course connections when they are clear.
limitations: Works best with complete lesson Markdown files.
Does not replace the original lesson explanation.
May omit connections when prerequisites are unclear.
Requires user review for accuracy and tone.

use_cases.title: Create Student Review Notes
use_cases.description: Turn a finished lesson into a short summary for quick study and recall.

use_cases.title: Refresh Existing Lesson Summaries
use_cases.description: Update summaries after lesson edits so key concepts stay aligned.

use_cases.title: Standardize Curriculum Summaries
use_cases.description: Apply the same section structure across lessons in a course module.

prompt_templates.title: Summarize One Lesson
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner use with a single lesson file.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to generate a summary for this lesson file: [path]. Keep the output concise and student focused.

prompt_templates.title: Refresh a Summary
prompt_templates.scenario: Update an existing companion summary after lesson changes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review [lesson path] and its existing summary. Update the summary so it reflects the current lesson content.

prompt_templates.title: Check Summary Quality
prompt_templates.scenario: Validate a generated summary before publication.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this lesson summary against the skill checklist. Identify missing concepts, overlong sections, and anti-patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Summarize a Module
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate summaries for these lesson files: [paths]. Keep each summary consistent with the required structure and length guidelines.

output_examples.input: A beginner Python lesson about data types.
output_examples.output: A short summary with a core concept, mental models, critical patterns, common mistakes, and connections.

output_examples.input: An updated lesson that now includes AI collaboration advice.
output_examples.output: A refreshed summary that adds the relevant collaboration pattern without exposing internal course framework labels.

output_examples.input: A lesson with unclear prerequisite links.
output_examples.output: A concise summary that omits connections when they would not help the student.

best_practices: Provide the complete lesson file, including frontmatter.
Review the generated summary before publishing it.
Keep summaries focused on recall, not re-teaching.
anti_patterns: Do not include full lesson explanations.
Do not paste large code examples into the summary.
Do not add motivational filler or hidden framework labels.

faq.question: What kind of files does this skill summarize?
faq.answer: It is designed for educational lesson files written in Markdown.

faq.question: Does it create a separate summary file?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow creates a `.summary.md` companion file for the lesson.

faq.question: Can it summarize advanced lessons?
faq.answer: Yes. It uses longer target ranges for advanced lessons when the frontmatter indicates higher proficiency.

faq.question: Does it include code examples?
faq.answer: No. The skill says summaries should reference patterns, not copy full code examples.

faq.question: Can it review existing summaries?
faq.answer: Yes. It can refresh summaries when lesson content changes.

faq.question: Does it require network access?
faq.answer: No evidence of network access was found in the reviewed skill file.
